2603.20112.title: Demonstration of Adapt4Me: An Uncertainty-Aware Authoring Environment for Personalizing Automatic Speech Recognition to Non-normative Speech
2603.20112.paper_summary: Personalizing Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for non-normative speech remains challenging because data collection is labor-intensive and model training is technically complex. To address these limitations, we propose Adapt4Me, a web-based decentralized environment that operationalizes Bayesian active learning to enable end-to-end personalization without expert supervision. The app exposes data selection, adaptation, and validation to lay users through a three-stage human-in-the-loop workflow: (1) rapid profiling via greedy phoneme sampling to capture speaker-specific acoustics; (2) backend personalization using Variational Inference Low-Rank Adaptation (VI-LoRA) to enable fast, incremental updates; and (3) continuous improvement, where users guide model refinement by resolving visualized model uncertainty via low-friction top-k corrections. By making epistemic uncertainty explicit, Adapt4Me reframes data efficiency as an interactive design feature rather than a purely algorithmic concern. We show how this enables users to personalize robust ASR models, transforming them from passive data sources into active authors of their own assistive technology.
2603.20112.paper_summary_zh: 個性化自動語音識別（ASR）對於非標準語音仍然具有挑戰性，因為數據收集需要大量人力，且模型訓練在技術上相當複雜。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了Adapt4Me，一個基於網絡的去中心化環境，該環境實現了貝葉斯主動學習，使端到端個性化得以在無需專家監督的情況下進行。該應用程序通過三階段的人機協作工作流程，向普通用戶展示數據選擇、適應和驗證：（1）通過貪婪音素抽樣進行快速檔案建立，以捕捉特定說話者的聲學特徵；（2）使用變分推斷低秩適應（VI-LoRA）進行後端個性化，以實現快速、增量的更新；以及（3）持續改進，用戶通過低摩擦的 top-k 修正來解決可視化的模型不確定性，指導模型的精煉。通過使認知不確定性變得明確，Adapt4Me 將數據效率重新框架為一種互動設計特徵，而不僅僅是純粹的算法問題。我們展示了這如何使用戶能夠個性化穩健的 ASR 模型，將其從被動數據源轉變為自己輔助技術的主動創作者。
2603.20112.author: Niclas Pokel et.al.
2603.20112.authors: Niclas Pokel, Yiming Zhao, Pehuén Moure, Yingqiang Gao, Roman Böhringer
